frameworks.name: intrinsic functional connectivity
frameworks.one_line: spatially organized macroscale correlations of spontaneous cortical activity fluctuations

frameworks.name: ascending neuromodulation
frameworks.one_line: subcortical systems with widespread cortical projections shape large-scale dynamics

frameworks.name: arousal
frameworks.one_line: global state variable linking brainstem output to cortical correlation structure

core_question: How do subcortical neuromodulatory systems shape the strength, topography, and topology of intrinsic correlations of large-scale cortical activity?
key_assumptions: intrinsic fluctuations unfold on a backbone of long-range anatomical connections
ascending neuromodulators are positioned to broadcast state signals cortex-wide
psychiatric and neurological disorders involve altered cortical dynamics tractable via neuromodulation
manipulations: review scope spans human, monkey, and rodent studies
emphasis on non-invasive recordings of human brain activity
key_findings: neuromodulatory systems reshape strength, topography, and topology of intrinsic correlations across time
shared and antagonistic principles emerge across distinct neuromodulator systems
altered intrinsic correlation patterns map onto Parkinson's disease, depression, and schizophrenia
interpretations: charting receptor- and neuromodulator-specific effects can constrain models of healthy brain function
principles of neuromodulatory control of intrinsic dynamics may seed mechanism-informed biomarkers and individualized treatments
limitations_noted: review is structured but explicitly selective rather than exhaustive
specific neuromodulator- and receptor-resolved effects remain to be charted
